data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-256af8b5">
    <title>S15-7: Multi-node trust attestation forwarding (Story-14 coupling)</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
BLOCKING for any non-localhost write enablement. In the coordinator topology (Story 14), the originating peer identity attestation is not forwarded from coordinator to client — any non-localhost write goes through the hub without carrying who initiated it. The spoke cannot authorize on both origin and coordinator identity because neither is transmitted.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/federation/fanout.go: current federation supports query fan-out only. No RouteMutationToProjectOwner, no ForwardMutation, no identity header propagation.
- Risk ('Multi-node authorization gap'): A compromised or misconfigured hub can forward mutations on behalf of any origin, with the spoke unable to distinguish legitimate coordinator-forwarded requests from hub-originated attacks. Without dual authorization, spoke trusts the hub implicitly.
- The /tmp/story-15-final.md plan file cited in the original bead is ephemeral and not available to sub-agents on other machines; relevant content is inlined below.

PLAN-LOCKED DECISIONS (from story-15 planning):
- Multi-node routing (point 6): hub picks single owning spoke by project ID from FederationRegistry; no broadcast of mutations.
- Identity forwarding: X-DDx-Origin-Identity = verified WhoIs peer at coordinator edge (NOT coordinator self); X-DDx-Coordinator-Identity = hub's own attested identity. Both mandatory on forwarded mutations.
- Dual authorization on spoke: requireTrusted augmented to check BOTH origin (WhoIs / loopback policy) AND coordinator (registered federation hub); 403 if either fails.

This bead has been decomposed into 4 children:
- ddx-468d800f — S15-7a: ADR-conformance lint for mutation resolvers.
- ddx-eb75a32d — S15-7b: Coordinator→client mutation routing envelope (foundation).
- ddx-ba31fec8 — S15-7c: Origin identity attestation forwarding + dual authorization.
- ddx-ea1ada47 — S15-7d: Integration test with two in-process servers.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT spec; changes go into FEAT-010 and FEAT-011 extensions.
- Performance optimization of federation routing.
- Story 14 query fan-out behavior (already shipped in fanout.go).
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Coordinator forwards origin identity (X-DDx-Origin-Identity) unchanged in mutation envelope from ddx-eb75a32d.
2. Client-side authorization (ddx-ba31fec8) checks both origin and coordinator identity; returns 403 when either missing or untrusted.
3. ADR-conformance lint (ddx-468d800f) added to CI and fails on any new mutation resolver missing requireTrusted or CSRF/Origin checks.
4. TestRequireTrusted_ForwardedMutation_AuthorizesOriginAndCoordinator passes (happy path).
5. TestFederation_OperatorPromptSubmit_CoordinatorToSpoke_IdentityUnchanged (ddx-ea1ada47) exercises full stack.
6. Three negative sub-tests (untrusted-coordinator, missing-origin, tampered-origin) all return 403.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... ./internal/federation/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
decomposed into ddx-468d800f (ADR-conformance lint), ddx-eb75a32d (mutation routing envelope), ddx-ba31fec8 (origin identity forwarding + dual auth), ddx-ea1ada47 (integration test). Original 4 ACs spanned federation infrastructure that does not yet exist (mutation routing — current federation only supports query fan-out), identity propagation, CI lint tooling, and an E2E test — too large for one execute-bead pass.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, story:15, kind:multi-node, blocking, triage:needs-investigation</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-1d85c927" base-rev="66f3634a6ae27cec804cb367fea29216b692f8b6" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260505T053837-43d90cfd"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260505T053837-43d90cfd/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260505T053837-43d90cfd/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260505T053837-43d90cfd/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260505T053837-43d90cfd/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260505T053837-43d90cfd/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>
